reported_cost.unit: sum of cost of goods sold, administration expenses, exploration and development expenses, share based payments, impairment, and finance costs (excl. depreciation)

notes: reported_cost calculated as sum of cost of goods sold (53,816,899), administration expenses (1,110,751), exploration & development expenses (1,125,626), share based payments (3,871,886), impairment (3,138,254), and finance costs (789,159), minus interest income (306,052 included in admin? No, removed). Total = 53,816,899 + 1,110,751 + 1,125,626 + 3,871,886 + 3,138,254 + 789,159 = 63,852,575. Wait, instructions: 'reported_cost means TOTAL operating costs for the period EXCLUDING depreciation: if the income statement lists components, sum those components and note the addends in its unit field; do NOT include depreciation, financial items or taxes.' So cost of goods sold includes depreciation & amortisation (as seen on page 22). We must exclude impairment, finance costs, and depreciation. Then add only operating components: From page 28, cost of goods sold (53,816,899) includes depreciation. Admin (1,110,751), exploration (1,125,626), share based payments (3,871,886). Sum = 53,816,899 + 1,110,751 + 1,125,626 + 3,871,886 = 59,925,162. But depreciation is inside COGS and not separately stated. However, EBITDA = 29.63M, Revenue = 82.93M, so Operating costs excl. dep = 82.93 - 29.63 = 53.30M. No, EBITDA = revenue - op costs (excl dep). On page 22, EBITDA = 29.63M. Revenue = 82.93M. So operating costs = 82.93 - 29.63 = 53.30M. This aligns with 'Underlying EBITDA' which is already provided. So reported_cost = 82,927,090 - 29,630,000 = 53,297,090? But page 4 says cost of goods sold was $53,816,899 and admin costs $1,110,751, exploration $1,125,626. That sums to 56,053,276. EBITDA from p22 is 29.63M, so operating costs = 82.927 - 29.63 = 53.297M. But sum of COGS + Admin + Exploration = 56.05M. That's a discrepancy. Underlying EBITDA excludes impairment, share based payments, etc? Wait, p22 'Underlying EBITDA' may exclude non-recurring items? It says 'Underlying EBITDA 29.63'. The income statement p28 shows gross profit = 29,110,191. Then deduct admin, share based payments, exploration, impairment, finance costs. Those sums are: admin (1,110,751), share based payments (3,871,886), exploration (1,125,626), impairment (3,138,254), finance costs (789,159). Total = 10,035,676. Plus other income (18,014). So profit before tax = 29,110,191 + 18,014 - 10,035,676 = 19,092,529. EBITDA before these items would be Gross Profit minus 'Other' admin etc before depreciation? But depreciation is inside COGS. So to get EBITDA, we take Gross Profit and deduct only admin and exploration? But share based payments and impairment are also operating? The instruction says reported_cost is TOTAL operating costs EXCLUDING depreciation. Sum of COGS (includes depreciation) + Admin + Exploration? That is not excluding depreciation. Since we cannot extract depreciation from COGS, and the instruction says sum the components but exclude depreciation, financial items, or taxes, we are stuck. However, we have 'Underlying EBITDA' given as 29.63M. EBITDA = Revenue - OpEx (including COGS ex depreciation, admin, exploration). So OpEx = Revenue - EBITDA = 82,927,090 - 29,630,000 = 53,297,090. But is this 'reported_cost'? The instruction: 'reported_cost means TOTAL operating costs for the period EXCLUDING depreciation: if the income statement lists components sum those components and note the addends in its unit field'. The income statement components: Cost of goods sold (includes dep), Admin, Exploration. Can't separate dep. Better: use 'Underlying EBITDA' to derive OpEx. Underlying EBITDA on p22 is 29.63M. So total OpEx ex dep = 82.93 - 29.63 = 53.30M. But this doesn't match sum of components. I'll use the sum of COGS + Admin + Exploration (53,816,899 + 1,110,751 + 1,125,626 = 56,053,276) and note it includes depreciation. But the instruction forbids including depreciation. So I'll compute OpEx as Revenue - Gross Profit + Admin + Exploration? No. Actually, EBITDA = Gross Profit + Other Income - Admin - Exploration? Not exactly. Typically EBITDA = Revenue - COGS (ex D&A) - SG&A. Since I don't have COGS ex D&A, I'll use Underlying EBITDA as given. So reported_cost = Revenue - Underlying EBITDA = 82,927,090 - 29,630,000 = 53,297,090. I'll set value = 53297090, unit = 'Revenue - Underlying EBITDA', page 22, confidence medium. Head grade: p18 gives 1.01 and 1.25 for two quarters. Average = (1.01+1.25)/2 = 1.13 g/t. Not explicitly an average for the half year, but derived. Recovery: p18 gives 85% and 88%. Average = 86.5%. AISC not reported explicitly. Cash cost not reported. Equity ratio not stated. Personnel expenses not stated as total number, only admin employment expenses 649,984 (p33), but not total. Ore mined: only BCM provided, not tonnes. Gold sold oz = 13,900 (p22). Gold produced = 14,194 oz (p5).
